proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall, unstratified).
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have different mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.728 mo on enzalutamide vs 3.755 off (n_on=20076). Welch t-test p=0.153.
Mean PFS 3.829 mo on olaparib vs 3.735 off (n_on=5098). p=0.0033.
Mean PFS 3.805 on pembro vs 3.741 off (n_on=2384). p=0.137.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib produces a larger increase in pfs_months among patients with brca2_mutation=1 than among patients with brca2_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab produces a larger increase in pfs_months among patients with msi_high=1 than among patients with msi_high=0.
treatment_lu177_psma produces a larger increase in pfs_months among patients with psma_high=1 than among those with psma_high=0.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by tp53_mutation status.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: in BRCA2+ patients olaparib gives +1.55 mo PFS (5.22 vs 3.67), in BRCA2- patients olaparib gives -0.07 mo (3.67 vs 3.74). Interaction term coef +1.62 (p=4e-61).
Interaction term coef -0.34, p=0.15. No significant differential pembrolizumab effect by MSI-H status (likely under-powered: only 79 MSI-H patients on pembro).
Interaction term coef -0.017, p=0.74. No differential Lu-177-PSMA effect by PSMA expression.
Interaction coef -0.04, p=0.63. No differential effect by TP53.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Patients with mcrpc=1 have shorter pfs_months than mcrpc=0 patients.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than visceral_mets=0 patients.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher log(ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher log(psa_ng_ml+1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
ar_v7_positive=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than ar_v7_positive=0.
Bone, liver, and adrenal metastases are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG slope -1.16 mo per unit; mean PFS 4.68 (ECOG 0), 3.49 (1), 2.38 (2). p<<1e-300.
mCRPC: 3.51 vs 4.03 mo, diff -0.52 mo, p=1.6e-181.
Visceral mets: 3.71 vs 3.75 mo, diff -0.05 mo, p=0.034 (small but significant).
Albumin slope +0.495 mo per g/dL, p=1.5e-166.
log(LDH) slope -0.078 (univariate), p=0.0027. Adjusted for other prognostics, slope is -0.097, p<1e-100.
log(PSA+1) slope -0.382 mo per unit log, p<<1e-300.
AR-V7+ vs neg: 3.73 vs 3.75 mo, diff -0.013, p=0.58. No prognostic signal.
bone_mets diff +0.011 (p=0.62); liver_mets -0.002 (p=0.95); adrenal_mets +0.042 (p=0.17). No site-specific prognostic effect except visceral_mets composite.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months in this cohort (positive slope) — opposite of clinical expectation.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher log(alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher inflammation markers (nlr, crp_mg_l) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater symptom burden (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, appetite_loss_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Age slope +0.174 mo per year (p<<1e-300, R²=0.73). Strong positive — older patients have markedly longer PFS, opposite of clinical prior.
Weight loss slope -0.075 mo per percentage point, p=1e-225.
log(ALP) slope -0.054 (p=0.008), borderline; not significant in multivariable model adjusted for other prognostics.

analyses.code: Univariate OLS for nlr and crp_mg_l
analyses.result_summary: NLR slope +0.002 (p=0.58); CRP slope -0.0006 (p=0.51); log(CRP+1) slope +0.003 (p=0.82). No detectable prognostic signal from these inflammation markers.
fatigue_grade p=0.66, pain_nrs p=0.80, dyspnea_grade p=0.81, appetite_loss_grade p=0.91. No measurable association between patient-reported symptom grades and PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories.
In a multivariable model adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), visceral_mets, weight loss, log(LDH), log(ALP), and hemoglobin, mcrpc remains an independent negative predictor of pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway across race groups; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(race_ethnicity) + covariates')
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across white/black/hispanic/asian/other: means within 0.06 mo, p=0.69. No race effect even after covariate adjustment (all p>0.67).
Means within 0.03 mo across medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured (p=0.79). No insurance effect adjusted (all p>0.88).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age + ecog + mcrpc + albumin + log_psa + visceral + weight_loss + log_ldh + log_alp + hgb + ...')
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, mcrpc coefficient is +0.0001 (p=0.97) — its univariate effect is fully explained by collinear prognostics (likely PSA, ECOG, albumin). MV model achieves R²=0.94 even before treatment terms.

proposed_hypotheses.text: AR-V7-positive patients have a smaller (or negative) treatment_enzalutamide and treatment_abiraterone effect on pfs_months than AR-V7-negative patients (predicted resistance).
tp53_mutation modifies the pfs effect of any of treatment_enzalutamide / abiraterone / docetaxel / olaparib.
pten_loss modifies the pfs effect of treatment_enzalutamide and treatment_abiraterone (PI3K/AR crosstalk).
prior_chemotherapy modifies the pfs effect of treatment_docetaxel.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt * ar_v7_positive', data=df).fit() for enz and abi
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide × AR-V7 interaction p=0.957 (coef +0.003); abiraterone × AR-V7 p=0.334 (coef +0.048). No detectable AR-V7 resistance signal.
All 4 interaction p-values >0.09 (enz p=0.18, abi p=0.094, doc p=0.25, olap p=0.50). No significant TP53 modifier of any treatment.
Enzalutamide × pten p=0.40 (coef +0.071); abiraterone × pten p=0.25 (coef +0.105). Direction nominally positive but not significant.
All three drug × prior_chemo interactions p>0.31; smallest p=0.31 for abiraterone (coef -0.040). No predictive signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each major comorbidity (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, heart failure, CAD, AFib, VTE, autoimmune, hepatitis, HIV, prior malignancy, depression/anxiety, ILD) is independently associated with pfs_months.
pleural_effusion or pericardial_effusion is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-test for each binary comorbidity vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All 15 comorbidities: every p>0.06 (smallest = depression_anxiety_diagnosis p=0.061, diff -0.045 mo). No comorbidity reaches conventional significance, none would survive multiple-comparison correction. Comorbidities appear noise in this synthetic cohort.
Pleural effusion diff -0.043 (p=0.087); pericardial effusion diff -0.036 (p=0.43). Neither significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one SNP among the 25 pharmacogenomic variants is associated with pfs_months at conventional p<0.05 (and survives Bonferroni correction at 0.05/25=0.002).

analyses.code: Univariate OLS of pfs_months on each SNP
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p=0.014 for snp_rs4986893 (coef -0.092). After Bonferroni (threshold 0.002), no SNP reaches significance. SNP main effects appear absent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one SNP × treatment interaction (across 25 SNPs × 6 treatments = 150 tests) shows a meaningful pharmacogenomic predictive effect on pfs_months at Bonferroni-corrected significance (p<0.05/150≈3.3e-4).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt * snp', data=df).fit() across 150 combinations
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p = 8.3e-4 (treatment_olaparib × snp_rs1799983, coef -0.21). Even this does not survive Bonferroni (3.3e-4). All other interactions p>0.01. No credible pharmacogenomic SNP × drug interaction in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, mean pfs_months on treatment_olaparib is at least 1 month longer than off treatment_olaparib.
In brca2_mutation=0 patients, treatment_olaparib has no benefit (and may be slightly harmful) on pfs_months.
treatment_lu177_psma improves pfs_months in patients with both psma_high=1 and bone_mets=1 (the indicated population).
Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ on olaparib: 5.22 mo (n=529); off: 3.67 mo (n=4467). Diff +1.545 mo (>1 mo threshold), p=4e-27.
BRCA2- on olaparib: 3.67 mo (n=4569); off: 3.74 mo (n=40435). Diff -0.073 mo, p=0.019. Olaparib non-beneficial (and slightly harmful) in BRCA2-.
In PSMA+/bone+ subgroup (n=6041): Lu177-PSMA on=3.78, off=3.76, diff +0.024, p=0.75. No detectable benefit.
MSI-H on pembro: 3.53 mo (n=79); off: 3.79 mo (n=1449). Diff -0.265, p=0.24. No measurable pembrolizumab benefit even in MSI-H, and the point estimate is opposite the expected direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG performance status modifies the pfs effect of any of the six treatments (treatment effect attenuated in higher ECOG patients).
mCRPC status modifies treatment effect — castration-resistant patients respond differently than hormone-sensitive patients to one or more drugs.
visceral_mets status modifies treatment effect on pfs_months for one or more treatments.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt * ecog_ps') for each of 6 trts
analyses.result_summary: All six treatment × ECOG interaction p-values between 0.29 and 0.66. No treatment effect modification by ECOG.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt * mcrpc') for each of 6 trts
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p=0.042 (treatment_docetaxel × mcrpc, coef +0.080); does not survive multiple-testing correction. Other interactions p>0.18.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt * visceral_mets') for each of 6 trts
analyses.result_summary: treatment_olaparib × visceral_mets interaction coef -0.178, p=0.017 — olaparib's benefit is reduced in visceral mets patients. Other treatments p>0.09.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), visceral_mets, log(LDH), and weight_loss_pct_6mo, the treatment_olaparib effect within brca2_mutation=1 is still ≥1 month and significant.
The treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction coefficient remains highly significant when fit jointly with major prognostic covariates.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib + age + ecog + albumin + log_psa + visceral + log_ldh + weight_loss', data=df[df['brca2_mutation']==1]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In adjusted BRCA2+ model, treatment_olaparib coef = +1.43 mo, p<1e-50. Confirms the subgroup effect is not confounded.
Interaction term coef +1.43, p<1e-300. Main olaparib and BRCA2 effects collapse to ~0; the entire signal is in the interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the rare molecular alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss) shows a main-effect association with pfs_months.
At least one prior therapy variable (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) is associated with pfs_months.
education_years and rural_residence are associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for each binary alteration
analyses.result_summary: All 13 alterations p>0.10. Smallest = cdkn2a_loss diff -0.042 (p=0.10). None survives Bonferroni. Rare alterations have no measurable prognostic effect on PFS in this cohort.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for each prior-therapy flag
analyses.result_summary: All five p>0.11. Smallest = prior_surgery (p=0.12, diff -0.03). Prior therapies not associated with PFS.

analyses.code: OLS on education_years; t-test on rural_residence
analyses.result_summary: education_years p=0.40 (coef -0.003); rural_residence p=0.50 (diff +0.015). No SDoH signal beyond race/insurance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong positive age_years → pfs_months association is independent of (uncorrelated with) standard prognostic covariates — i.e., it is not explained by older patients having less aggressive disease.
The Pearson correlation between age_years and pfs_months is large (|r|>0.5).

analyses.code: Mean covariates across age strata; multivariable OLS retaining age_years
analyses.result_summary: Age has near-zero correlation with albumin (r=0.003), log(PSA) (r<0.001), log(LDH) (r=0.005), and ECOG/mcrpc/visceral_mets distributions are uniform across age. After full multivariable adjustment, age coef remains +0.174 mo/yr (p<<1e-300). Age effect is structural, not confounded.
Pearson r = 0.856 (p≈0). Age is by far the dominant individual predictor of PFS in this synthetic cohort, an unrealistic pattern relative to real-world prostate-cancer epidemiology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among vital signs and additional labs (systolic_bp_mmhg, diastolic_bp_mmhg, heart_rate_bpm, spo2_pct, tsh_uiu_ml, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, glucose_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, inr, ca_125_u_ml, cea_ng_ml), at least one is associated with pfs_months at the Bonferroni-adjusted threshold (≈0.003).

analyses.result_summary: Smallest p=0.032 (glucose_mg_dl coef +0.0007; total_bilirubin coef -0.062 p=0.032). Neither survives Bonferroni (~0.003). All others p>0.08. No additional lab/vital prognostic signal beyond established markers (LDH, ALP, albumin, hemoglobin, PSA).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment (each of the six treatments) is balanced across baseline patient characteristics (age, ECOG, albumin, log_PSA, log_LDH, visceral_mets, mcrpc, brca2, msi_high, psma_high) at p>0.01 — consistent with non-confounded (randomization-like) assignment.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests / proportion comparisons of each baseline covariate by each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Across 6 treatments × 10 covariates = 60 comparisons, only 3 reached p<0.05 (treatment_abiraterone vs visceral_mets diff -0.009, p=0.022; treatment_docetaxel vs log_LDH diff -0.009, p=0.013; treatment_pembrolizumab vs ECOG diff -0.032, p=0.024) — all near-trivial differences and consistent with chance after multiple comparisons. Treatment assignment is essentially balanced — interpretable as randomized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a three-way interaction treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × visceral_mets on pfs_months, such that the BRCA2+ olaparib benefit is concentrated in patients without visceral metastases and abolished (or reversed) in patients with visceral metastases.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+/no visceral mets: olaparib +1.92 mo (n_on=432, p=2e-31). BRCA2+/visceral mets: olaparib -0.13 mo (n_on=97, p=0.58). Three-way interaction term coef = -2.09, p=9.4e-17. Strong evidence visceral metastases abolish the BRCA2-olaparib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model with age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), log(LDH), log(ALP), visceral_mets, mcrpc, weight_loss_pct_6mo and the treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × visceral_mets terms explains >95% of variance in pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: R²=0.976, adj R²=0.976. After adjustment, log_alp, visceral_mets and mcrpc lose all main-effect signal (p>0.3), confirming they were proxies for other prognostics. Olaparib×BRCA2 stays at +1.75 mo (p<<1e-300); 3-way interaction -1.73 (p<<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cumulative gene_burden (sum of tp53_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, keap1_mutation, cdkn2a_loss) is associated with shorter pfs_months, and may modify treatment_pembrolizumab effect (TMB-like surrogate).
Within msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by visceral_mets status.

analyses.result_summary: Gene burden main effect coef -0.012 (p=0.29). Pembrolizumab × burden interaction coef -0.088 (p=0.10). Neither significant.

analyses.code: Stratified mean comparison within msi_high=1 by visceral_mets
analyses.result_summary: MSI-H/no visceral: pembro on n=66, mean 3.42 vs off 3.81, diff -0.39 (p=0.10). MSI-H/visceral: pembro on n=13, mean 4.06 vs off 3.72, diff +0.34 (p=0.60). Tiny sample, no significant differential effect; adjusted interaction p=0.996.

proposed_hypotheses.text: psma_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (better-differentiated tumor proxy).
There is a treatment_lu177_psma × psma_high × bone_mets three-way interaction on pfs_months (Lu-177-PSMA benefits the indicated PSMA+/bone-met population).

analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high mean PFS 3.75, PSMA-low 3.73, diff +0.02, p=0.27. No PSMA prognostic effect.
Three-way interaction coef +0.241, p=0.063 (borderline). Within PSMA+/bone+ subgroup: Lu177 diff +0.024 mo (p=0.75). No clinically meaningful Lu-177-PSMA benefit emerges in any subgroup tested.

proposed_hypotheses.text: AR-V7+ patients have attenuated benefit from treatment_enzalutamide and treatment_abiraterone vs AR-V7- patients (testing the canonical biological hypothesis).
PTEN-loss patients have attenuated benefit from treatment_enzalutamide vs PTEN-intact patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction smf.ols('pfs_months ~ enz*ar_v7 + abi*ar_v7 + covariates')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enzalutamide × AR-V7 coef +0.002 (p=0.87); abiraterone × AR-V7 coef +0.006 (p=0.63). Stratified subgroup differences <0.04 mo. The classic AR-V7 resistance signal is not present in this synthetic dataset.
Enzalutamide effect in PTEN-loss vs intact: +0.041 vs -0.030 mo, interaction p=0.62. No PTEN modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for major prognostic covariates, race_ethnicity and insurance_type are still independently associated with pfs_months (residual disparity).
Treatment receipt rates (proportion receiving each of the six treatments) differ across race_ethnicity and insurance_type categories (access disparity).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(race_ethnicity) + covariates') and same for insurance
analyses.result_summary: All race coefficients within ±0.003 of reference (p>0.67). All insurance coefficients within ±0.001 (p>0.88). No residual SDoH disparity in PFS.

analyses.code: Group-mean treatment-receipt rates by race & insurance
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-receipt rates differ by ≤2.6 percentage points across all race and insurance categories for every treatment (e.g., enzalutamide ranged 0.383–0.421; pembrolizumab 0.037–0.051). Small magnitudes, no signal of access disparity in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Ranking by univariate R², age_years > ecog_ps > log(PSA) > weight_loss_pct_6mo > albumin_g_dl > mcrpc > log(LDH) > visceral_mets in explaining pfs_months variance.
An incremental model adding age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), log(LDH), weight loss, visceral_mets, mcrpc reaches R²>0.96, and adding olap*brca2*visceral terms increases R² further to ~0.976.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate R²: age=0.732, ecog=0.151, log_psa=0.053, weight_loss=0.020, mcrpc=0.016, albumin=0.015, log_ldh=0.0002, visceral_mets=0.0001. Hypothesized ordering largely correct except albumin ranks below mcrpc (very close).

Cumulative R² after adding age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), log(LDH), weight loss, visceral, mcrpc: 0.969. Adding olaparib×BRCA2×visceral terms raises R² to 0.976. Adding the other 5 treatment main effects adds essentially nothing (R² stays 0.976). Treatments other than the BRCA2-olaparib axis explain ~0 variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among 5 (non-olaparib) treatments × 8 biomarkers × 3 disease-extent modifiers = 120 three-way interaction tests, at least one shows a Bonferroni-significant (p<4.2e-4) effect.

analyses.code: Loop over 120 trt × biomarker × modifier 3-way interaction OLS models
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p = 0.013 (treatment_docetaxel × tp53_mutation × visceral_mets, coef -0.247). None of the 120 reach Bonferroni significance (4.2e-4). The treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × visceral_mets interaction remains the only credible 3-way signal in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final multivariable OLS model adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), log(LDH), weight loss, and the olaparib × BRCA2 × visceral_mets and pembrolizumab × MSI-H terms: (a) age, ECOG, albumin, log(PSA), log(LDH), and weight loss are each highly significant prognostic factors with the expected signs (positive for age and albumin in this cohort, negative for ECOG, log(PSA), log(LDH), weight loss); (b) the only treatment-related coefficients with non-trivial magnitude and significance are the BRCA2 × olaparib interaction (positive, large) and the BRCA2 × olaparib × visceral_mets interaction (negative, large); (c) the pembrolizumab × MSI-H interaction is null.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age + ecog + albumin + log_psa + log_ldh + weight_loss + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation*visceral_mets + treatment_pembrolizumab*msi_high', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final model R²=0.976, n=50000. Coefficients: age +0.174 (p=0), ecog -1.151 (p=0), albumin +0.478 (p=0), log_psa -0.383 (p=0), log_ldh -0.097 (p=2e-128), weight_loss -0.077 (p=0), treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation +1.750 (p<<1e-300), treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation:visceral_mets -1.726 (p<<1e-300), treatment_pembrolizumab:msi_high +0.014 (p=0.70). All claims (a), (b), (c) supported.
